<!-- Tool-owned: regenerated by MoNecromanCI on every command. Do not edit ‚Äî changes will be overwritten. -->

# MoNecromanCI ‚Äî your monorepo's grimoire

> **MO**no(repo) + **NECROMAN**cy + **CI**. The CLI command is `monecromanci`
> (short alias `mnci`). This document is written into the repo by the tool and
> kept up to date every time you run any of its commands.

MoNecromanCI created (or adopted) this NX monorepo and keeps its configuration
alive. Everything you need to work in this repo day to day is described below.

---

## Philosophy

1. **One root, all dependencies.** Every dependency of every project lives in
   the root `package.json`. Projects never carry their own `node_modules`;
   version conflicts between sibling projects are impossible, and upgrading a
   dependency is a single change. Publishable libraries still ship **real,
   resolved dependencies** in their published `dist/package.json` ‚Äî a script
   traces what each lib actually imports at build time.
2. **Tool-owned vs scaffold.** Every generated file has an owner:
   - **Tool-owned** files (tsconfigs, `nx.json`, `eslint.config.mjs`, Jest
     presets, `project.json`, pipelines, this document, ‚Ä¶) are regenerated
     verbatim by the tool. Never edit them ‚Äî run `monecromanci doctor --fix`
     (or `update`) and they snap back to canonical. This is what makes config
     drift impossible.
   - **Scaffold** files (`package.json`, your `src/**`, `.env.*`, `README.md`,
     ‚Ä¶) are written **once, only if missing**. They are yours; the tool never
     touches them again.
3. **Convention over configuration.** Per-project config is 2‚Äì4 tiny files
   that mostly `extends` the root. Adding a project never requires editing
   the root config.
4. **Fast lint on purpose.** The ESLint flat config is **non-type-checked**
   (standard style/no-semi + @stylistic + typescript-eslint + unicorn +
   framework plugins + Jest + TSDoc + JSON/YAML/Markdown). Type errors are the
   compiler's job (`tsc --noEmit` per project); lint stays fast on large repos.
5. **Debugging must work on the TypeScript.** Source maps, source-resolved
   internal libs (`main ‚Üí src/index.ts`) and workspace-level VSCode launch
   configs are wired so breakpoints bind in `.ts`/`.tsx` ‚Äî including stepping
   from an app into a lib.
6. **Nothing is sacred.** A repo that wasn't born from MoNecromanCI can be
   brought under management with `resurrect` ‚Äî it detects your projects, asks
   before believing its own guesses, and never touches your sources.

## Commands

Run **bare** `monecromanci` (or `mnci`) for an interactive menu of everything
below. Each command also has a necromancy alias ‚Äî use whichever reads better:

| Command                        | Alias       | What it does |
| ------------------------------ | ----------- | ------------ |
| `monecromanci new [name]`      | `summon`    | Scaffold a brand-new monorepo (prompts: CI provider, registry, scope, base branch, initial lib). Scriptable: `--yes --ci github --registry github-packages --owner acme`. |
| `monecromanci add [type]`      | `conjure`   | Add a project: `internal-lib`, `publishable-lib`, `cli-tool`, `function-app`, `node-app`, `react-app`, `vue-app`, `svelte-app`, `nextjs-app`. |
| `monecromanci resurrect`       | `adopt`     | Adopt an existing monorepo: detect projects under `apps/`/`libs/`, confirm each kind, pick which to manage, apply the canonical config. Re-run anytime to adopt the rest. |
| `monecromanci doctor [--fix]`  | `raise`, `fix` | Compare every tool-owned file against canonical and check dependency health (superseded lint packages, missing `legacy-peer-deps`); report issues (and repair them with `--fix`). |
| `monecromanci update`          | `ascend`    | `doctor --fix` + re-stamp the template version ‚Äî run after upgrading MoNecromanCI itself. |
| `monecromanci validate [--all]`| `ritual`    | Run `nx affected -t lint test build` locally (`--all` = every project) before pushing to CI. |
| `monecromanci spell`           | `scry`      | List the projects with uncommitted changes (grouped by `apps/`/`libs/`/root) plus a ready-made conventional-commit scope, e.g. `feat(jato.index,web): ‚Ä¶`. Also prints advisory ‚öÝ possible-breaking-change hints ‚Äî removed exports/changed signatures in publishable libs, `engines`/peer-dependency/`bin` contract changes ‚Äî and suggests the `!` marker when any are found. Hints are heuristic: renames across files can be false positives, and behavioural breaks are invisible to any diff ‚Äî the final call is yours. |
| `monecromanci spellbook`       | `grimoire`  | Write (or refresh) this `MoNecromanCi.md` guide at the repo root. |
| `monecromanci release`         | `foretell`  | Preview the next automated release ‚Äî fetches tags, then runs `nx release version --dry-run` to show the version bump each publishable project would get, without changing anything. |

## Versioning & releases (conventional commits)

Releases are driven entirely by your **commit messages** ‚Äî no manual version
edits. The pieces working together:

- **commitlint + husky**: the `commit-msg` hook rejects commits that don't
  follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org):
  `type(scope): subject`. Valid types include `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`,
  `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `build`, `perf`, `style`, `revert`.
- **nx release** reads the conventional commits that touched each publishable
  project since its last tag and computes the next version:

  | Commit                                              | Version bump          |
  | --------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
  | `fix(my-lib): handle empty input`                    | **patch** (x.y.Z)     |
  | `feat(my-lib): add streaming mode`                   | **minor** (x.Y.0)     |
  | `feat(my-lib)!: drop Node 20` or a footer line `BREAKING CHANGE: ‚Ä¶` | **major** (X.0.0) |
  | `chore`/`docs`/`refactor`/`test`/‚Ä¶                   | no release            |

- Projects version **independently** (`projectsRelationship: 'independent'`);
  only projects tagged `type:publishable-lib` (publishable libs and CLI tools)
  are released. Tags look like `my-lib@1.2.3` and each project gets its own
  `CHANGELOG.md`.

Release from the repo root:

```sh
npm run release            # version + changelog + publish, interactive
npm run release:version    # only compute versions + changelogs + tags
npm run release:publish    # only publish what was already versioned
```

See `docs/nx-release.md` for the registry-specific details (auth, first
publish, CI publishing).

### Adopting a repo with published history

`nx release` reads each project's current version from a **git tag** named
`{projectName}@{version}` (`release.releaseTag.pattern` in `nx.json`). A repo brought
in with `resurrect` from outside MoNecromanCI has none of those tags, so for any
project **already published to a registry**, nx would compute the next version
from the scaffold `0.0.0` and clash with the higher version already on the feed
‚Äî `npm publish` then refuses to move the `latest` tag backwards.

Seed a one-time baseline, once, per already-published project (skip projects
that were never published ‚Äî nx tags them on their first release):

```sh
# tag name uses the NX PROJECT name (project.json "name"), not the npm package name
git tag -a "atlassian@1.10.21" -m "release baseline"   # use each project's CURRENT published version
# ‚Ä¶repeat per published project‚Ä¶
git push origin --tags
```

Also set each project's `package.json` `version` to match its baseline tag (so
nothing publishes `0.0.0` in the meantime), then confirm with
`npx nx release version --dry-run` ‚Äî it should now compute from the real current
version instead of `0.0.0`. After this, conventional commits drive versions
normally.

## Generated monorepo structure

```text
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ apps/                        # deployable applications
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ <app>/                   #   project.json, tsconfig(s), src/, jest config
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ libs/                        # libraries (internal + publishable + CLI tools)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ <lib>/                   #   same shape; publishable libs add typedoc.json
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .configurations/             # (function/node apps) per-env Azure app settings
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .husky/                      # commit-msg hook ‚Üí commitlint
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .vscode is in *.code-workspace  # workspace file carries the debug configs
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/nx-release.md           # how releasing works against your registry
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ MoNecromanCi.md              # ‚Üê this document (tool-owned)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nx.json                      # caching, affected, release config (tool-owned)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ eslint.config.mjs            # re-exports monecromanci-toolchain's config (tool-owned)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ azure-pipelines.yml          # and/or .github/workflows/ci.yml, per your CI choice
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .npmrc                       # registry + auth-token placeholder (scaffold)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json                 # ALL dependencies + the scripts below (scaffold)
```

The `tsconfig.base.json`/`tsconfig.jest.json`/`jest.preset.mjs` (+ `setup`/
`clear`)/`typedoc.json` configs and the CI engine scripts/per-project helper
scripts all live in `node_modules/monecromanci-toolchain` ‚Äî a devDependency
of every generated repo ‚Äî instead of being vendored into it. Per-project
files (`tsconfig.json`, `jest.config.mjs`, `typedoc.json`) `extend`/`import`
from there.

Root scripts you will use daily:

```sh
npm run build|lint|test|doc          # nx run-many across every project
npm run build:affected|lint:affected|test:affected|doc:affected
npm run affected                     # lint + test + build for affected projects
npm run graph                        # visualise the project graph
npm run projects                     # list projects
npm run pipeline:plan                # dry-run what CI would do
```

## Project kinds

| Kind              | Nx tag                    | What you get |
| ----------------- | ------------------------- | ------------ |
| `internal-lib`    | `type:internal-lib`       | Source-resolved (`main ‚Üí src/index.ts`): debuggers step into it, "find references" works across libs. Never published. |
| `publishable-lib` | `type:publishable-lib`    | Built to `dist/` with declarations; `dist/package.json` gets real resolved deps; released by `nx release`. |
| `cli-tool`        | `type:publishable-lib`    | A publishable lib that also ships a bundled `bin` (esbuild). Shares its tag with `publishable-lib` ‚Äî both release the same way; the `bin` field is what tells them apart. |
| `function-app`    | `type:function-app`       | Azure Functions v4, per-env `.configurations/*.json`, attach debugging on `:9229`. |
| `node-app`        | `type:node-app`           | Framework-agnostic TS HTTP server ‚Äî extend with Express/Koa/Fastify/Nest/‚Ä¶; packaged like a function app. |
| `react-app`       | `type:react-app`          | Vite; `dev`/`uat`/`prod` builds to `dist-dev`/`dist-uat`/`dist-prod`; browser debugging on `:5173`. |
| `vue-app`         | `type:vue-app`            | Vue 3 + Vite, same multi-env builds. |
| `svelte-app`      | `type:svelte-app`         | Svelte 5 + Vite, same multi-env builds. |
| `nextjs-app`      | `type:nextjs-app`         | Next.js App Router; per-env builds in server (standalone) or static-export mode (`NEXT_OUTPUT`). |

Every project answers the same four targets ‚Äî `build`, `test`, `lint` and, for
backend/lib kinds, `doc` (typedoc) ‚Äî so `nx affected` composes them uniformly.
A project tagged `ci:ignore` instead is excluded from the pipeline entirely
(never built, packaged, versioned or published) ‚Äî useful for a project that
lives in the workspace but isn't ready to be managed yet.

## CI & registry

Chosen when the repo was created (or resurrected) and recorded in the stamp:

- **CI provider**: Azure DevOps Pipelines, GitHub Actions, or both. The
  `node_modules/monecromanci-toolchain/build-templates/*.mjs` scripts are the
  single engine; the YAML files are thin wrappers, so both providers run
  identical logic.
- **Registry**: Azure Artifacts, GitHub Packages, or public npm. The `.npmrc`,
  each publishable project's `publishConfig` and `docs/nx-release.md` are
  generated to match. Auth uses the `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable.

## The stamp: `.monecromanci.json`

The tool records how the repo was generated (workspace name, scope, CI,
registry, node version, template version) in `.monecromanci.json` at the root.
`doctor`, `update`, `add` and `validate` require it; `resurrect` creates it
for adopted repos. Commit it.

## Debugging

Open the `*.code-workspace` file (not the bare folder). It ships
breakpoint-capable configs at the workspace level: **Debug Jest (current
file)** (`--runInBand`, source maps), a Function/Node App **attach** config
(`:9229`), and browser configs for Vite (`:5173`) and Next.js (`:3000`). The
`Orta.vscode-jest` extension adds a Debug lens above each test.

## When something drifts

```sh
monecromanci doctor        # what changed vs canonical?
monecromanci doctor --fix  # put it back
monecromanci update        # after upgrading the tool: re-sync + re-stamp
```

Remember: if you need different behaviour in a tool-owned file, don't edit it ‚Äî
it will be regenerated. Extend at the edges the templates leave for you
(scaffold files, per-project `package.json` scripts, your sources).
